description: Lexer tokenizes @, #, <<, >>, ~@, ::, ++, --, ~, ?? operators with correct disambiguation
steps: Tokenize @ as TOKEN_AT and # as TOKEN_HASH
Tokenize << as TOKEN_LSHIFT and >> as TOKEN_RSHIFT
Tokenize ~@ as TOKEN_TILDE_AT and ~ as TOKEN_TILDE
Tokenize ++ as TOKEN_CONCAT and -- as TOKEN_REMOVE

description: Parser produces correct AST for index, index-set, push, pop, slice, concat, remove, contains, unique, length, remove-at
steps: Parse arr -- [5] into BinaryExpr with op --
Parse arr ?? [5] into BinaryExpr with op ??
Parse arr ~@ 2 into BinaryExpr with op ~@

description: Runtime supports Index, IndexSet, Length, Push, Pop, RemoveAt, Slice, Concat, Remove, Contains, Unique
steps: Index returns correct element wrapped as *SlopValue
IndexSet mutates array element at index
Pop removes and returns last element
RemoveAt removes and returns element at index
Slice returns sub-array from low to high
Concat returns new array with all elements of both
Remove returns new array with first occurrence removed
Contains returns [1] or [] based on membership

description: Codegen lowers IndexExpr, IndexSetStmt, PushStmt, PopExpr, SliceExpr and binary/unary array ops to sloprt calls
50+ e2e tests covering index, index-set, push, pop, remove-at, slice, concat, remove, contains, unique, length, and combined patterns
steps: Index: numeric index, nested array, out of bounds panic
Push: single element, empty array, inside loop
Pop: returns last, modifies array, empty panic
RemoveAt: first, middle, last, out of bounds panic
Slice: basic, from start, to end, empty slice, out of bounds panic
Contains: existing, non-existent, empty, in if condition
Unique: with duplicates, empty, already unique
Combined: arrays.slop roadmap test, push+iterate, filter pattern
Edge cases: chained operations, fn returning array + index
